UserAccount.title: UserAccount
UserAccount.description: Represents a user account within the SwiftRoute system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserAccount entity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was last updated.

Shipment.title: Shipment
Shipment.description: Represents a shipment being handled by SwiftRoute.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Shipment entity.

trackingNumber.description: The tracking number associated with the shipment.

status.description: The current status of the shipment (e.g., 'In Transit', 'Delivered').

origin.description: The origin location of the shipment.

destination.description: The destination location of the shipment.

expectedDeliveryDate.description: The expected delivery date of the shipment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the shipment record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the shipment record was last updated.

PreAlert.title: PreAlert
PreAlert.description: Represents a pre-alert request for a shipment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PreAlert entity.

receiptImageURL.description: URL of the receipt image associated with the pre-alert.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the pre-alert request was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the pre-alert request was last updated.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FlightManifest entity.

flightNumber.description: The flight number associated with the manifest.

shipmentIds.description: References to Shipments. (Relationship: Shipment N:N FlightManifest).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the flight manifest was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the flight manifest was last updated.

ProfitLossStatement.title: ProfitLossStatement
ProfitLossStatement.description: Represents a profit and loss statement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProfitLossStatement entity.

statementDate.description: The date for which the profit/loss statement is generated.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the P&L statement was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the P&L statement was last updated.

definition.description: Stores user account information.  Path-based ownership ensures only the authenticated user can access their own data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores shipment data associated with a specific user. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their own shipments.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: shipmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the shipment.

definition.description: Stores pre-alert data associated with a specific shipment for a specific user. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access pre-alerts related to their shipments.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: shipmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the shipment.

params.name: preAlertId
params.description: The unique identifier for the pre-alert.

definition.description: Stores flight manifest documents.  If access control is required beyond authentication, denormalize roles into the document for Authorization Independence.

params.name: flightManifestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the flight manifest.

definition.description: Stores profit and loss statements.  If access control is required beyond authentication, denormalize roles into the document for Authorization Independence.

params.name: profitLossStatementId
params.description: The unique identifier for the profit/loss statement.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability, adhering to the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, and QAPs. It uses path-based ownership for user-related data and denormalization for authorization contexts. The structure facilitates secure list operations and enforces data integrity through explicit state modeling and semantic naming.

Specifically:

*   **User Accounts:** Stored under `/users/{userId}`. This provides a clear, path-based ownership model for user data, ensuring only the user can access their own account information.
*   **Shipments:** Stored under `/users/{userId}/shipments/{shipmentId}`.  This enforces ownership, meaning only the user can access the shipments they created.  This also supports QAPs, as listing shipments under a user is now secure.
*   **PreAlerts:** Stored under `/users/{userId}/shipments/{shipmentId}/preAlerts/{preAlertId}`. Since PreAlerts are directly related to shipments, the structure continues the user-ownership pattern established for Shipments. This ensures that only users with access to a specific shipment can access its associated pre-alerts.
*   **Flight Manifests:** Stored under `/flightManifests/{flightManifestId}`.  No user-ownership is defined, thus exists at the top level.  If we did have roles, we would denormalize the access roles to the document, so we avoid the `get()` call in rules.
*   **ProfitLossStatements:** Stored under `/profitLossStatements/{profitLossStatementId}`.  No user-ownership is defined, thus exists at the top level.   If we did have roles, we would denormalize the access roles to the document, so we avoid the `get()` call in rules.

This structure achieves Authorization Independence by avoiding hierarchical authorization dependencies. Access control can be implemented using rules based solely on `request.auth.uid` and the document path, without needing to `get()` data from parent documents.  QAPs are supported by the segregation of data based on ownership and the use of membership maps (when applicable) to control access to collaborative data.
